headline: Former Xbox studios Double Fine and Compulsion will keep games after going indie
summary: Microsoft is spinning off four Xbox studios, including Double Fine and Compulsion. Double Fine and Compulsion will retain their existing game franchises post-independence. This move is part of Xbox's recent restructuring and layoffs.

headline: I spy
summary: The article critiques smart glasses, focusing on the cultural problem of privacy and surveillance. Netflix's 'A Man on the Inside' unintentionally highlights smart glasses' biggest issue. Hollywood is blamed for both setting and ruining expectations for smart glasses.

headline: The DJI Osmo Pocket 3 lookalike is down to $329
summary: The Xtra Muse, a camera similar to the DJI Osmo Pocket 3, is currently on sale for $329. This price is a better deal than the DJI Osmo Pocket 3, which is also discounted. The DJI Osmo Pocket 3 is now $378, down from its usual $500 price.

headline: Inside the big business of the creator economy, with the agents making it happen
summary: A Decoder podcast episode features Ali Berman and Raina Penchansky from United Talent Agency's Creators division. They discuss the thriving business of the creator economy and influencer marketing. The interview took place at the Cannes Lions advertising festival in France.

headline: Fans Mourn Starfield Amid Xbox Layoffs, With Bethesda Now Seemingly Focused on Fallout, Elder Scrolls, Wolfenstein, Doom, and Quake
summary: Fans are mourning Starfield's future following Xbox layoffs and a shift in Bethesda's focus. Bethesda appears to be prioritizing established franchises like Fallout and Elder Scrolls. Reports suggest a Starfield sequel is unlikely due to Xbox's recent restructuring.

headline: The 65" Samsung S90F 4K QD-OLED TV Drops to $1,208 with Free Shipping on Amazon
summary: The 65-inch Samsung S90F 4K QD-OLED TV is now available for $1,208 on Amazon. This deal includes free shipping, making it a compelling offer. The S90F was previously named the best gaming TV in 2026 by the reviewing source.

headline: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered’s Physical Switch 2 Release Comes on a Cartridge – Here’s Where You Can Preorder It
summary: Preorders are now live for The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered Physical Deluxe Edition. The game will be available on a cartridge for the Nintendo Switch 2. It is set to be released on August 11, with preorder options detailed in the article.

headline: As Xbox Tries to Sell or Close Arkane, the Future of Marvel's Blade Remains Unclear, With Reports Suggesting It Was Delayed Internally and Is Running Over Budget
summary: The future of Marvel's Blade game is uncertain as Xbox considers selling or closing developer Arkane Lyon. Reports indicate the game has faced internal delays. The project is also reportedly running over its allocated budget.

headline: Trump confirms he asked Fifa boss for review of US striker's ban
summary: Donald Trump confirmed he asked Fifa to review Folarin Balogun's one-match ban. Trump stated Fifa 'made the right decision' by suspending the ban. He believed the ban would have put a 'big stain' on the World Cup.

headline: Henderson expected to have surgery on injured wrist
summary: Jordan Henderson is anticipated to undergo surgery for a wrist injury. The injury occurred while celebrating England's victory over Mexico. The incident happened on Monday, leading to the need for medical intervention.

headline: Buckingham Palace says Prince Harry will not stay there despite his team announcing he would
summary: Buckingham Palace has clarified Prince Harry will not be staying there, contradicting his team's earlier announcement. Royal sources indicate Harry did not respond to an accommodation offer by a set deadline. This news affects his reported plans for an upcoming visit.

headline: 'The spectacle Iran wants the world to see': Lyse Doucet in Tehran for supreme leader's funeral
summary: Huge crowds gathered in Tehran for the funeral procession of Iran's late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. BBC's Lyse Doucet reported from the scene, describing the massive public display. The event was framed as a spectacle intended for a global audience.
